currentService.enumeration: nine team root files, flat inbox/review directories, recursive .opencode-runtime and members trees, app attachment trees, and flat task JSON
currentService.asyncPath: process-local team promise mutex; validates/enumerates then copies files independently; per-file errors may log and continue
currentService.shutdownPath: separate synchronous enumeration/copy implementation with broader error swallowing
currentService.identityMutation: best-effort _backupIdentityId patch into CLI-owned config.json; identity can rotate on resurrection
currentService.publication: manifest and thin registry are independent atomic file replacements; there is no immutable stage or commit marker
currentService.restore: config-validity gate, identityId heuristic, per-file validity/mtime partial restore, and automatic full restore after missing/corrupt config
currentService.prune: removes per-file stale copies and old team directories without an ADR-32 committed-run invariant
currentService.coordinationFailure: does not fence provider/CLI writers and does not include SQLite Online Backup API

faultMatrix.fault: missing required config name followed by a valid config
faultMatrix.observedResult: invalid config blocks backup; valid config yields matching async/sync enumeration and mutates _backupIdentityId to the manifest identity

faultMatrix.fault: async enumeration reports an error and one injected source disappears before copy
faultMatrix.observedResult: stale-file prune is suppressed, the failed file is absent, other changed files copy, and manifest/registry publication continues

faultMatrix.fault: stale backup file after an error-free enumeration
faultMatrix.observedResult: the stale file is removed before the independently published manifest/registry sequence

faultMatrix.fault: async manifest atomic publication throws after file copies
faultMatrix.observedResult: backupTeam rejects; copied config remains while manifest and registry entry are absent

faultMatrix.fault: async registry atomic publication throws after manifest publication
faultMatrix.observedResult: backupTeam rejects; manifest exists while on-disk registry is absent and the in-memory registry is ahead

faultMatrix.fault: shutdown enumeration includes one missing file beside a valid changed file
faultMatrix.observedResult: sync copy swallows the missing-file error, copies the valid file, and still publishes manifest/registry
the per-team shutdown wrapper swallows the failure and the outer shutdown path still saves the thin registry
initialize rebuilds the active in-memory registry from independently readable per-team manifests

faultMatrix.fault: source config and team directory are missing while a valid backup manifest/config exists
faultMatrix.observedResult: initialize performs a full restore and activates copied config and team metadata

faultMatrix.fault: source config is corrupt with both newer and older non-config source files
faultMatrix.observedResult: full restore replaces config, preserves a newer source file by mtime, and overwrites an older source file

faultMatrix.fault: identity-matching valid config with one valid, one corrupt, and one missing JSON participant
faultMatrix.observedResult: partial restore preserves valid JSON and restores corrupt/missing JSON independently

faultMatrix.fault: one retention-expired and one recent deleted-team backup
faultMatrix.observedResult: prune deletes the expired directory/registry entry, retains the recent entry, and publishes registry independently

requiredProducts.promise: consistent app-owned SQLite snapshot plus explicitly named identity/coordination participants only
requiredProducts.writerRequirement: fence/flush app participants; unrelated provider writers may remain active

requiredProducts.promise: quiesced verified publication across all catalogued non-ephemeral state
requiredProducts.writerRequirement: stop/refuse uncoordinated writers, close observation watermarks, freeze generations
requiredProducts.restoreEligibility: only committed v2 input for replace_deployment

limitations: The fixture characterizes existing TeamBackupService behavior; it does not make the independent file copies a coordinated recovery point.
Provider replacement between stat/read/copy remains covered by the separate external-writer negative fixture, not by an unsafe real provider launch.
Final-image SQLite ABI, async worker wire operation, deployment quiescence, and immutable recovery-point publication remain W6/controller gates outside this remediation lane.
